positive_feedback: IN1: detect-knowledge-updates.py compares source MD5 against registry state, supports all/since/dry-run and is covered by test_detect_knowledge_updates; goal-setting records are excluded at the consumer boundary.

OUT1/C1-C3: the revised phase table assigns 20-file batching to the orchestrator and classification to knowledge-extractor, matching actual responsibility. Six-category schema/router/registry updates, split thresholds and relation candidate→validated atomic graph merge have distinct owners.

C4: the corrected schema note states extractor writes schema-compliant entries while info-collector reads data via router, avoiding a false direct schema dependency. All referenced scripts, agents, knowledge files and RUNBOOK path exist; UBM pytest 199 PASS.
negative_feedback: low: progress/intermediate/handoff wiring remains dense in one bullet, but paths and invariants are unambiguous.

notes: Iteration 1 found two ownership/dependency descriptions that did not match the implementation; iteration 2 corrected them. Four elegance conditions PASS.
